instruction: I am stuck on my roof. Water is rising fast. I have two children with me.
context: Disaster: Flood, Location: Rural Bihar, India
response:
Immediate Actions: Stay on the roof and do not attempt to swim. Wave a bright cloth to signal rescuers. Keep children tethered to you. Blow a whistle in 3 short bursts repeatedly.
Shelter: Do not enter a closed attic as you could be trapped. Stay on the highest visible point.
Supplies: Grab clean water, a flashlight, and a whistle if reachable.
Contacts: Shout or whistle in 3-burst intervals. This is the universal distress signal.

instruction: The forest near my farm is on fire. The wind is blowing towards us.
context: Disaster: Wildfire, Location: Nashik Outskirts, India
response:
Immediate Actions: Evacuate immediately. Do not wait for an official order. Drive away from the smoke, windows up. Wet any cloth and hold it over nose and mouth.
Shelter: If trapped, find an area clear of vegetation or a body of water. Lie face down and cover your body.
Supplies: N95 mask or wet cloth. Car keys, ID, water, and first aid kit.
Contacts: Honk your horn continuously while driving to warn neighbors.

instruction: There is a strong smell of gas after the earthquake.
context: Disaster: Earthquake, Location: Urban Area
response:
Immediate Actions: Do not use matches, lighters, or any electrical switches. Open all windows and doors immediately. Leave the building without using the elevator.
Shelter: Move to an open area away from buildings, power lines, and trees.
Supplies: First aid kit, sturdy shoes, battery-powered radio, and documents.
Contacts: Tap on a pipe or wall in 3-beat patterns so rescuers can locate you.

instruction: We are experiencing extreme heat and have run out of water.
context: Disaster: Heatwave, Location: Rajasthan, India
response:
Immediate Actions: Move to shade or the coolest room immediately. Wet your clothing with any available liquid. Fan yourself or others constantly. Do not exert energy.
Shelter: Stay in a single room on the ground floor with closed curtains. Ground floors are cooler.
Supplies: Mix oral rehydration salts (ORS) or salt and sugar in any water available. Use wet cloths on wrists, neck, and forehead.
Contacts: Inform local Panchayat or a community health worker of anyone showing confusion or stopping sweating.

instruction: We hear loud cracking sounds from the hillside above our village. Soil is moving.
context: Disaster: Landslide, Location: Darjeeling Hills, India
response:
Immediate Actions: Move away from the slope immediately. Run perpendicular to the slide direction, not downhill. Alert neighbors by shouting.
Shelter: Take refuge in a solid building far from the base of the hill. Avoid valleys and low ground.
Supplies: Grab emergency bag with documents, water, and a flashlight. Wear sturdy footwear.
Contacts: Wave from high visible ground. Use a mirror or torch to signal if help does not arrive.

instruction: Cyclone is approaching our coastal village. Winds are very strong.
context: Disaster: Hurricane, Location: Coastal Odisha, India
response:
Immediate Actions: Shut all windows and doors. Move to the strongest interior room away from windows. Lie flat if the roof begins to shake.
Shelter: Go to a designated cyclone shelter if reachable within 30 minutes. If not, stay in your strongest room.
Supplies: Fill containers and bathtubs with water now before supply fails. Keep food, torch, and radio within reach.
Contacts: After the eye passes and calm feels temporary, do not go outside. The back wall of the storm is coming.

instruction: Our river has dried up completely. Crops are failing. No water for drinking.
context: Disaster: Drought, Location: Marathwada, India
response:
Immediate Actions: Conserve all available water strictly. Stop all non-essential water use immediately. Collect morning dew from leaves using cloth if needed.
Shelter: Limit time outdoors especially between 11am and 4pm to reduce dehydration.
Supplies: Prioritize drinking water for children and elderly. Boil or treat any alternative water source before consuming.
Contacts: Contact local government drought relief cell or Red Cross for emergency water tanker support.

instruction: Flash floods are sweeping through our community. Roads are blocked.
context: Disaster: Flood, Location: Sylhet, Bangladesh
response:
Immediate Actions: Move immediately to upper floors. Do not try to cross any flowing water even if it looks shallow. Six inches of fast water can knock you over.
Shelter: Stay above ground level. If on a roof, signal with bright colored cloth.
Supplies: Emergency water, torch, whistle, and any dry food. Waterproof your documents in a plastic bag.
Contacts: Tune a battery radio to emergency broadcasts. Signal with mirror flashes toward any helicopter or boat.

instruction: A volcano near our town is erupting. Ash is falling heavily.
context: Disaster: Volcanic Eruption, Location: Mount Sinabung area, Indonesia
response:
Immediate Actions: Cover your nose and mouth with a damp cloth or N95 mask immediately. Seal doors and windows with wet towels or tape. Evacuate if an official order is given.
Shelter: Stay indoors in a sealed room. Thick ash can collapse roofs so avoid weak structures.
Supplies: Goggles for eye protection against ash. Water for drinking and cloth dampening. Sturdy shoes.
Contacts: Monitor community emergency radio. Do not return until authorities confirm the eruption has stabilized.

instruction: We have no food or clean water for three days. There is no help in sight.
context: Disaster: General Emergency, Location: Remote Region, Sub-Saharan Africa
response:
Immediate Actions: Ration all remaining food and water strictly. Children and the sick get priority. Collect any rainwater in clean containers.
Shelter: Conserve energy by resting in shade. Avoid heat exposure to reduce water loss.
Supplies: Eat small amounts every 4 to 6 hours to preserve energy. Boil water for at least 1 minute before drinking. Search for roots, edible plants, or morning dew.
Contacts: Use any reflective material such as a mirror or foil to signal passing aircraft. Lay rocks or sticks in a large X pattern in an open clearing.

instruction: A major earthquake has collapsed buildings. Many people are trapped.
context: Disaster: Earthquake, Location: Kathmandu, Nepal
response:
Immediate Actions: Check yourself for injuries before helping others. Do not enter damaged buildings. Aftershocks will come. Move to open ground.
Shelter: Stay away from collapsed structures and overhead lines. Set up outdoors if weather permits.
Supplies: First aid for bleeding wounds. Use cloth strips as bandages. Do not move anyone with a possible spinal injury.
Contacts: Assign one person to shout and listen every 5 minutes. Trapped persons should tap rhythmically on pipes to guide rescuers.

instruction: There is a tsunami warning. We are in a coastal area.
context: Disaster: Tsunami, Location: Coastal Tamil Nadu, India
response:
Immediate Actions: Do not wait. Move immediately inland and uphill. Do not stop to collect belongings. If you can see the ocean receding unusually, run immediately without waiting for an alarm.
Shelter: Move to high ground at least 30 meters above sea level or 3 kilometers inland. If not possible, go to the top floor of a strong concrete building.
Supplies: Nothing matters except your life right now. Move first, everything else later.
Contacts: Help elderly and children move first. Call out to anyone near the shore to run inland.

instruction: Several children and elderly have severe diarrhea and vomiting after flood.
context: Disaster: Flood Aftermath / Disease, Location: Rural Bangladesh
response:
Immediate Actions: Boil all water for at least 1 full minute before drinking or cooking. Stop eating any food that contacted floodwater.
Shelter: Isolate sick individuals to prevent spread. Wash hands with soap or ash after contact.
Supplies: Make Oral Rehydration Solution: 1 liter boiled water, 6 teaspoons sugar, half teaspoon salt. Give small sips every few minutes.
Contacts: Alert the nearest health post or community health worker immediately. Cholera spreads fast in flood conditions.

instruction: Ice storm has knocked out all electricity. We have no heat.
context: Disaster: Winter Storm, Location: Ontario, Canada
response:
Immediate Actions: Move everyone into one small interior room to preserve body heat. Block door gaps with towels. Do not burn charcoal or use gas stoves indoors.
Shelter: The smallest room retains heat best. Layer all available clothing including hats and gloves.
Supplies: Candles provide warmth and light. Use body heat by wrapping everyone in blankets together. Store snow water inside to melt for drinking.
Contacts: Signal from a window with a flashlight at night. Hang a bright cloth outside when safe to do so.

instruction: Bushfire is surrounding our rural property. We cannot evacuate now.
context: Disaster: Wildfire, Location: Victoria, Australia
response:
Immediate Actions: Move to your shelter-in-place room now. Fill sinks, baths, and buckets with water. Wet all exterior walls if a hose is available. Seal gaps under doors with wet towels.
Shelter: Choose a room with the fewest windows on the opposite side of the approaching fire. Lie low under windows.
Supplies: Woolen blankets for protection from radiant heat. Torch, water, wet cloth masks. Stay hydrated.
Contacts: When fire front passes in roughly 30 minutes, check for spot fires inside. Emerge carefully and signal emergency services.

instruction: A chemical spill has happened near our neighborhood. There is a strange smell.
context: Disaster: Chemical Hazard, Location: Industrial Zone
response:
Immediate Actions: Do not go toward the source. Move crosswind or upwind away from the smell. Cover nose and mouth with a wet cloth immediately.
Shelter: Shelter in place inside a sealed building if evacuation is not possible. Turn off all air conditioning and ventilation.
Supplies: Seal windows and vents with tape or damp cloths. Do not eat or drink anything that may have been exposed.
Contacts: Do not use open flames or electrical switches near the spill area. Wait for official all-clear before going outside.

instruction: 我们被洪水困住了，无法撤离。
context: Disaster: Flood, Location: Guangdong, China
response:
Immediate Actions: Stay on the highest floor. Do not attempt to swim through floodwater. Wave a bright colored item toward rescue helicopters.
Shelter: Avoid the rooftop in lightning. Stay at the highest interior level.
Supplies: Store drinking water in containers now. Keep identification documents in a waterproof bag.
Contacts: Use a flashlight to signal SOS in 3 short, 3 long, 3 short bursts toward any rescue craft.

instruction: hamnachukua maji machafu na watoto wanaougua.
context: Disaster: Drought/Disease, Location: Rural Kenya
response:
Immediate Actions: Boil all water immediately for at least one minute. Do not give unboiled water to children.
Shelter: Rest in shade to reduce dehydration. Limit physical activity in heat of day.
Supplies: Make oral rehydration solution with boiled water, sugar, and salt. Give sips every 10 minutes to sick children.
Contacts: Inform the nearest community health volunteer or local clinic of sick children immediately.

instruction: Nuestra casa fue arrasada por el huracán. No tenemos refugio.
context: Disaster: Hurricane, Location: Puerto Rico
response:
Immediate Actions: Find the nearest solid concrete structure immediately. Stay low and away from windows and metal objects.
Shelter: A concrete stairwell or interior bathroom offers the best protection in a damaged structure.
Supplies: Purify water by boiling or using bleach drops. Avoid flooded roads and downed power lines.
Contacts: Once safe, hang a visible cloth signal from your location so relief teams can find you.

instruction: We are in a refugee camp and a violent storm is approaching.
context: Disaster: Storm, Location: Cox's Bazar, Bangladesh
response:
Immediate Actions: Secure tent pegs and ropes immediately. Move any unsecured items inside. Identify the nearest solid building in the camp.
Shelter: If winds become extreme, abandon the tent and shelter behind or inside any solid structure even a concrete wall.
Supplies: Keep water containers sealed. Prepare emergency bag: documents, any medications, water.
Contacts: Follow instructions from camp management and UN protection staff immediately without delay.
